title: CleanSpark Earnings Report: AI Infrastructure Pivot Amid Mining Pressure
text: CleanSpark will report fiscal Q3 results Thursday with analysts anticipating a loss of $0.41 per share on revenue of $152.32 million for the quarter ending March 31. While this represents improvement from the prior quarter's $1.35 per-share loss, sequential revenue is projected to decline 16% from $181.2 million, reflecting challenging conditions in cryptocurrency mining economics. All 12 analysts tracking the stock maintain Strong Buy ratings with an average price target of $19.29, suggesting 44% upside from the current $13.41 share price. Notably, earnings estimates have climbed 24.59% over 60 days despite revenue projections declining 7.52%, indicating confidence in improved profitability through business model transition. According to Needham analyst commentary, CleanSpark is in advanced discussions with an investment-grade hyperscaler regarding its Georgia facility, with negotiations having notably progressed during fiscal 2026. Needham raised its price target to $18 from $17 based on increased confidence regarding a potential near-term lease agreement. The company controls over 1.8 gigawatts of power capacity across multiple locations, including newly approved Texas facilities. Management has signaled intentional slowdown of Bitcoin mining expansion to prioritize capital allocation toward artificial intelligence and high-performance computing infrastructure. CleanSpark missed expectations in Q1, posting a $1.35-per-share loss driven by non-cash valuation adjustments and revenue of $181.2 million versus $194.1 million consensus expectations. This disappointment sparked significant stock decline and intensified scrutiny regarding diversification beyond pure mining operations. Trading near $13.41, down 43% from its 52-week high of $23.61, investors require concrete evidence that hyperscaler discussions translate into binding agreements capable of stabilizing revenue and expanding margins.
